enumeratedConstants.name: SPP_IDLE_PACKET
enumeratedConstants.annotation: Per Space Packet Standard, all 1s (11bits) is reserved for Idle Packets

enumeratedConstants.name: INVALID_UNINITIALIZED
enumeratedConstants.annotation: Anything equal or higher value is invalid and should not be used

typeDefinitions.annotation: APIDs are 11 bits in the Space Packet protocol, so we use U16. Max value 7FF
The stage of the file read operation

enumeratedConstants.name: SILENCED
enumeratedConstants.annotation: No logic applied. Does not send group and freezes counter.

enumeratedConstants.name: EVERY_MAX
enumeratedConstants.annotation: Send every MAX ticks between sends.

enumeratedConstants.name: ON_CHANGE_MIN
enumeratedConstants.annotation: Send on updates after MIN ticks since last send.

enumeratedConstants.name: ON_CHANGE_MIN_OR_EVERY_MAX
enumeratedConstants.annotation: Send on updates after MIN ticks since last send OR at MAX ticks between sends.

typeDefinitions.annotation: Enumeration for rate logic types for telemetry groups
The type of a parameter identifier
An enumeration for Version Type

enumeratedConstants.name: INVALID_UNINITIALIZED
enumeratedConstants.annotation: Anything equal or higher value is invalid and should not be used

typeDefinitions.annotation:
Transfer Frame Version Numbers are 4 bits long
CCSDS References add 1 to the binary value when counting versions in english (e.g. TM & TC are "version 1" but 0b00)
Until the release of USLP, TFVN were 2 bits long
With the addition of USLP two bits were added to the trailing end that serve as the 2 most significant bits
(i.e. on the wire USLP sends 0b1100)
See section 3.2.2.2 of USLP Overview (CCSDS 700.1-G-1),
section 4.1.2.2.2 of AOS (CCSDS 732.0-B-5),
section 4.3.2 of Space Data Link Protocol Summary (CCSDS 130.2-G-3),
& table 3-1 in Overview of Space Comms Protocols (CCSDS 130.0-G-4)
Array of queue depths for Fw::Buffer types

min.annotation: Minimum Sched Ticks when in ON_CHANGE_MIN

max.annotation: Maximum Sched Ticks when in EVERY_MAX

typeDefinitions.annotation: Parameter read error

enumeratedConstants.name: PARTIAL
enumeratedConstants.annotation:
The partially transmitted state
A data product is in this state from the start of transmission
until transmission is complete.

typeDefinitions.annotation: The type of a data product priority
Protocol IDs for EPP encapsulation packets per CCSDS 133.1-B-3 Section 4.1.2.3.3

The type of smaller indices internal to the software, used
for array indices, e.g., port indices. Must be signed.

Severity level for event filtering
Similar to Fw::LogSeverity, but no FATAL event

enumeratedConstants.annotation: Indicates time is processor cycle time. Not tied to external time

enumeratedConstants.name: TB_SC_TIME
enumeratedConstants.annotation: Time as reported by the spacecraft clock.

commands.name: ComCcsds.comQueue.FLUSH_QUEUE

formalParams.name: indexType
formalParams.annotation: The index of the queue (within the supplied type) to flush

commands.annotation:
Flush a specific queue. This will discard all queued data in the specified queue removing it from eventual
downlink. Buffers requiring ownership return will be returned via the bufferReturnOut port.

commands.name: ComCcsds.comQueue.FLUSH_ALL_QUEUES
commands.annotation:
Flush all queues. This will discard all queued data removing it from eventual downlink. Buffers requiring
ownership return will be returned via the bufferReturnOut port.

events.name: DataProducts.dpBufferAccumulator.BA_BufferAccepted

events.name: DataProducts.dpBufferAccumulator.BA_QueueFull
events.annotation: The Buffer Accumulator instance received a buffer when its queue was full. To avoid uncontrolled sending of events, this event occurs only when the previous buffer received did not cause a QueueFull error.
